assumptions.note: If your intensity encoding is opposite, flip more_covered_operator.

items.raw_intent: Central areas of Ipswich attract more tourists and activities, so I expect the central region to have higher travel-option coverage than the outer areas (outer areas are still covered, just less).
items.question: Are central regions more covered than outer regions (with outer still non-zero covered)?

items.raw_intent: Most outer areas should be less covered than the center, but the right-upper and bottom outer parts should be more covered than other outer parts because of universities and tourist attractions in those zones.
items.question: Are most outer regions less covered than the center, but upper-right and bottom-outer regions more covered than other outer regions?

items.raw_intent: In this bar chart, I expect the bars on the right side to represent higher values than the bars on the left side, because the categories are ordered from low to high.
items.question: Are the right-side regions (proxy for bars on the right) more covered than left-side regions?

items.raw_intent: In this line plot, I expect an increasing trend from left to right, because the metric should grow over time.
items.question: Is there a left-to-right increase in activity (proxy: right side more covered than left side)?

items.raw_intent: In this scatter plot, I expect the upper half to be denser than the lower half, because higher values are more frequent in the dataset.
items.question: Is the upper half denser/more covered than the lower half?
